questionHtml: Outline why electrons with energy of approximately 107 eV would be unsuitable for the investigation of nuclear radii.
markschemeHtml:
the de Broglie wavelength of electrons is «much» longer than the size of a nucleus ✓


hence electrons would not undergo diffraction
OR
no diffraction pattern would be observed ✓

meta.title: Outline why electrons with energy of approximately 107 eV would be unsuitable for the investigation of nuclear radii.
